steps.title: Use the 45-45-90 side pattern
steps.content: An isosceles right triangle has two equal legs. If each leg is $a$, the hypotenuse is $a\sqrt{2}$, so the perimeter is $a+a+a\sqrt{2}=a(2+\sqrt{2})$.

steps.title: Set up the perimeter equation for a 45-45-90 triangle
steps.content:
An isosceles right triangle has legs of equal length. Let's call the leg length $a$.

In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse equals $a\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is: $$2a + a\sqrt{2} = a(2 + \sqrt{2}) = 86 + 86\sqrt{2}$$

steps.title: Match the leg length to the perimeter
steps.content: If $a=43\sqrt{2}$, then the perimeter is $$43\sqrt{2}(2+\sqrt{2})=86\sqrt{2}+86$$ This is the same as the given perimeter $86+86\sqrt{2}$. Therefore the leg length is $43\sqrt{2}$, so B is correct.
